key_insight: A plasma-sprayed nano Al2O3–TiO2 coating on AISI 1020 steel improved corrosion resistance, with the 4 wt.% Al2O3 + 2 wt.% TiO2 coating performing best.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing ceramic coating composition and exposure conditions can substantially reduce corrosion in mild-steel components used in aggressive saline environments.
method: Definitive Screening Design (DSD) varied Al2O3 content, NaCl concentration, exposure time, temperature, and pH.
Potentiodynamic polarization and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) measured corrosion behavior.
Statistical modeling identified the most influential factors and quantified response trends.
evidence_signal: The best coating reached a corrosion current density of 2.18 µA cm−2 and the models reported R² values above 0.99, indicating strong predictive fit.
watch_item: Results are specific to the tested spray conditions and electrolyte environment, so scale-up or different service environments may change the coating’s performance.
paper_title: STUDY ON THE CORROSION BEHAVIOUR OF PLASMA SPRAYED NANO Al2O3–TiO2– AISI 1020 STEEL COATINGS USING DEFINITIVE SCREENING DESIGN
